sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, a 54-year-old male, presents with a 2-3 month history of stabbing, shooting pain in the right cheek bone area of his face, which can be triggered by certain temperatures and cold winds.
He reports that the pain is severe, rating it a 10 out of 10, and can last from a few minutes to an hour.
He denies numbness or tingling in the affected area and has no weakness on the right side of his face.
He has tried ibuprofen and Motrin with no relief.
He has a history of occasional headaches but no migraines.
He denies a history of multiple sclerosis or brain tumors.
Past medical history: NOT MENTIONED
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in the affected area and has no weakness on the right side of his face.
He denies any other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: NOT MENTIONED
Objective:
Vital signs: NOT MENTIONED
Physical exam: The patient's smile seems equal on both sides, and he has no weakness in his eyes or vision.
The patient can hear a sound on both sides of his face when a sound is made next to his ear.
The patient reports a little bit of numbness on the right side of his face when cotton tape is run along the side of his face.
The patient reports that the left side of his face feels sharp when poked with a needle, while the right side feels dull.
Test Results: The patient's MRI shows a small blood vessel abutting and perhaps pinching the trigeminal nerve.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a condition called trigeminal neuralgia, where the compression of the trigeminal nerve by a small blood vessel causes shooting electrical pain in the face.
Problem list: Trigeminal neuralgia; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Tegretol for the patient's trigeminal neuralgia, with a plan to follow up in a month to assess the efficacy of the medication.
We will also perform genetic testing to ensure the medication is safe for the patient.
Follow-up: The patient will return in one month to assess the effectiveness of the medication and discuss further treatment options if necessary.
